verses.text: I am Paul. God chose me to be an apostle of Christ Jesus. I am writing this letter to the believers who live in the city of Ephesus and who are faithful followers of Christ Jesus.
I, Paul pray that God our Father and the Lord Jesus Christ will give you grace and peace.
We praise God the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, because He is very good to us. We belong to Christ and the Holy Spirit has given us all the good things in heaven.
Before God made the world, He had chosen us to his family through Christ. To be holy and full of love and without fault when He looks at us.
And before the world was made, God decided to make us his own children through Jesus Christ. This was what God wanted, and it pleased him to do it.
So we praise God for the wonderful grace that He has given us because we belong to his Son, whom He loves very much.
Christ gave his blood and his life. Christ died for us and that is how God has forgiven our sins and made us free. This is how God has shown us how wonderful his grace is.
God let us know his secret plan. This was what God wanted, and God planned on doing it through Christ.

Regard to the fulfillment of the times, the end of history, God plan is to bring all things together in Christ, both things in the heavens and things on the earth. All to be joined together with Christ as the head.
In Christ we have received an inheritance. God had already planned for us to be his people, through faith in Christ, because that is what God wanted.
We who were the first to hope in Christ, who first put our trust in Jesus as our Lord and Savior, would exist to the praise of His glory.
You in Ephesus also belong to Christ, because someone has told you the true Good News that God wants to save you, and you believe it. God gave you the Holy Spirit, that He promised to give to us.
The Holy Spirit is the first payment that guarantees of the inheritance until the saved of God’ all gets to heaven. The goal for all God children is the praise of God in all his glory.
Someone has told me that you believe in the Jesus and that you love everyone who belongs to God.
That is why, when I, Paul, pray, I always thank God for you and I think of you in my prayers.
I, Paul also ask the God of our Lord Jesus Christ, the Father of glory, to give you spiritual wisdom and show you how you can get to know Him better.

I, Paul, pray that God will open your minds to see his truth. Then you will know the hope that he has chosen us to have. You will know that the blessings God has promised his holy people are rich and glorious.
I, Paul pray that you will know how strong God’s power is in the lives of those of us who believe in Him. It is the same as the mighty power of God.
This is the same power that God used when He made Christ live again and let Jesus sit on God right side in heaven.
Christ is more important than everyone and everything that wants to rule over this world and more important than anyone we can think of, now and in the time to come.
God put everything under Christ’s power and made him head over everything for the church.
The church is Christ’s body. Christ makes the church live and gives true life to the church and to all.
You did God make alive, because you were spiritually dead because of your sins and the things you did against God.

You lived like the sinful people of this world, and you did what the devil wanted you to do. The devil is the leader of the evil spirits. The devil wants to rule the people of the world, and he is the one who works in the hearts of the people who do not want to listen to God.

In the past all of us lived like that, trying to please our sinful selves. We did all the things our bodies and minds wanted. Like everyone else in the world, we deserved to suffer God’s anger just because of the way we were.
But God has a lot of mercy that He wants to give. God loves us and He has shown it to us.
We were spiritually dead because of all we had done against God. But God gave us new life together with Christ. You have been saved by God’s grace.
We belong to Christ Jesus and just like God made Christ live again. God will raise us from death and to sit seat us together with him in the heavenly places
God did this so that his kindness to us who belong to Christ Jesus would clearly show for all time to come the amazing richness of his grace.
You were saved by grace because you believed. You did not save yourselves; it was a gift from God
God has saved you, but God did not do it because you have done good things. This is why no one may boast and say that he is good.
God has made us what we are. In Christ Jesus, God made us new people so that we would spend our lives doing the good things he had already planned for us to do.
You must remember that you were Gentiles. The Jews said you were not circumcised. It is true that the Jews are circumcised, but it is only their bodies that have changed.

Remember that you did not belong to Christ before. The people of Israel were the people of God, not you. God made covenants with the people of Israel, not with you. God promised a lot of things to the people of Israel, not to you. You had no hope, and you did not know God
You were far away from God, but now you have come near to Him because Jesus Christ. You are brought near to God through the blood sacrifice of Christ.
Christ is our peace and Christ made us Jews and you who are not Jews one people. We were separated by a wall of hate that stood between us, but Christ broke down that wall. By giving his own body.
Christ death on the cross ended the hatred, and the law with its many commands and rules. Christ purpose was to make the two groups become one in him. By doing this he would make peace
Through the cross Christ ended the hate between the two groups. And after they became one body, he wanted to bring them both back to God. Christ did this with his death on the cross
Christ came and brought the message of peace to you non-Jews people who were far away from God. And Christ also brought that message of peace to those who were near to God
Through Christ we all have the right to come to God the Father in one Spirit
Now you non-Jewish people are not visitors or strangers, but you are citizens together with God’s holy people. You belong to God’s family

You are the church. You are like a building. The foundation of this building is the apostles and prophets, and Christ Himself is the most important stone in the building. Ephesians 3:21 To God be the glory in the church and in Christ Jesus for all time, forever and ever. Amen.
And in Christ you are being built together with his other people. You are being made into a place where God lives through the Spirit.
So I, Paul, am a prisoner because I serve Christ Jesus for you who are not Jews.
Surely you know that God gave me this work through his grace to help you.
God let me know his secret plan by showing it to me. I, Paul have already written a little about this.
When you read what I, Paul have written, you can understand the secret truth about Christ.
People who lived in other generations were not told that secret truth. But now, through the Spirit, God has made it known to his holy apostles and prophets.

And this is the secret truth: that by hearing the Good News, those who are not Jews will share with the Jews in the blessings God has for his people. They are part of the same body, and they share in the promise God made through Christ Jesus.
By God’s gift of grace. I, Paul became a servant to tell that Good News. God gave me that grace by using his power.
I, Paul am the least important of all God’s people. But he gave me this gift to tell the non-Jewish people the Good News about the riches Christ has. These riches are too great to understand fully
And God gave me the work of telling all people about the plan for his secret truth. That secret truth has been hidden in him since the beginning of time. God is the one who created everything
God purpose was that all the rulers and powers in the heavenly places will now know the many different ways he shows his wisdom. They will know this because of the church.
This agrees with the plan God had since the beginning of time. God did what he planned, and he did it through Christ Jesus our Lord.
In Christ we come before God with freedom and without fear. We can do this because of our faith in Christ.
So I, Paul ask you not to be discouraged because of what is happening to me. My sufferings are for your benefit and for your honor and glory.
That is why I, Paul kneel down and pray to the Father, of our Lord Jesus Christ
Every family in heaven and on earth gets its true name from our Lord Jesus Christ.
I, Paul ask the Father with his great glory to give you the power to be strong in your spirits. God will give you that strength through his Spirit
I, Paul pray that Christ will live in your hearts because of your faith. I pray that your life will be strong in love and be built on love
And I, Paul pray that you and all God’s holy people will have the power to understand the greatness of Christ’s love how wide, how long, how high, and how deep that love is.
Christ’s love is greater than anyone can ever know, but I, Paul pray that you will be able to know that love. Then you can be filled with everything God has for you.
With God’s power working in us, he can do much, much more than anything we can ask or think of.
To him be glory in the church and in Christ Jesus for all time, forever and ever. Amen.

4.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: So I, a prisoner because I serve the Lord, beg you to live in a way that matches the calling God gave you.
Be completely humble and gentle. Be patient, putting up with one another in love.
Work hard to keep the unity that the Spirit gives, by living together in peace.
There is one body and one Spirit, just as you were called to one hope.
There is one Lord, one faith, one baptism;
one God and Father of all, who is over all, works through all, and is in all.
But to each one of us Christ has given grace, in the amount he decided to give.
That is why the Scripture says: When he went up to heaven, he led the prisoners away, and he gave gifts to people.
(Now what does “he went up” mean, except that he also came down to the lower parts, the earth?
The one who came down is the same one who went up far above all the heavens, so that he might fill everything.)
And Christ gave gifts: some to be apostles, some prophets, some who tell the good news, and some pastors and teachers.
He gave these gifts to prepare God's people for service, so that the body of Christ would be built up,
until we all reach unity in the faith and in knowing the Son of God, and become mature — growing up to the full measure of Christ.
Then we will no longer be babies, thrown around and blown here and there by every new teaching, fooled by clever and tricky people who lie in wait to lead others the wrong way.
Instead, speaking the truth in love, we will grow up in every way into Christ, who is the head.
From Christ the whole body is joined and held together, each part doing its work. As each part does its share, the body grows and builds itself up in love.
So I tell you this, and I say it strongly in the Lord: do not keep living like people who do not know God, whose thinking leads nowhere.
Their minds are in the dark. They are cut off from the life God gives, because they refuse to understand and their hearts are hard.
They have lost all shame and have given themselves over to doing anything dirty, always wanting more.
But that is not the way you learned Christ.
Surely you heard about him and were taught the truth that is in Jesus.
You were taught to take off your old self, the way you used to live, which is being ruined by its deceiving desires.
You were taught to let your minds be made new,
and to put on the new self, created to be like God, truly right and holy.
So stop lying. Each of you must speak the truth to your neighbor, because we all belong to one body.
When you are angry, do not sin. Do not let the sun go down while you are still angry,
and do not give the devil a chance.
Anyone who has been stealing must steal no more. Instead, he must work, doing something useful with his own hands, so that he has something to share with people in need.
Do not let any rotten talk come out of your mouth. Say only what is helpful for building others up, so that it does good to those who hear.
And do not make the Holy Spirit of God sad. The Spirit is the mark that you belong to God, kept safe for the day he sets you free.
Get rid of all bitterness, bad temper, anger, shouting, and insults, along with every kind of hate.
Be kind and tender to one another, forgiving each other, just as God forgave you because of Christ.

5.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: So follow God's example, as his dear children.
Live a life of love, just as Christ loved us and gave himself for us, as a sweet-smelling offering and sacrifice to God.
Among you there must not be even a hint of sexual sin, or any kind of impurity, or greed, because these are not right for God's holy people.
There must be no dirty talk, foolish talk, or rude joking, which are out of place. Instead, give thanks.
You can be sure of this: no one who sins sexually, or is impure, or is greedy (which is the same as worshiping idols) has any place in the kingdom of Christ and of God.
Do not let anyone fool you with empty words. It is because of these things that God's anger comes on those who disobey him.
So do not have anything to do with them.
You once were darkness, but now you are light in the Lord. So live as children of light.
The light produces every kind of goodness, right living, and truth.
Find out what pleases the Lord.
Have nothing to do with the useless things that people do in the dark. Instead, show how wrong they are.
It is shameful even to talk about the things such people do in secret.
But when the light shines on these things, they are seen for what they are,
because light makes everything clear. That is why it is said: Wake up, sleeper. Rise from death, and Christ will shine on you.
So be very careful how you live. Do not live like fools, but like wise people.
Make the most of every chance you have, because these are evil days.
So do not be foolish, but understand what the Lord wants.
Do not get drunk on wine, which leads to wild living. Instead, be filled with the Spirit.
Speak to one another with psalms, hymns, and songs from the Spirit. Sing and make music in your hearts to the Lord.
Always give thanks to God the Father for everything, in the name of our Lord Jesus Christ.
Put others first, out of respect for Christ.
Wives, follow the lead of your husbands, as you follow the Lord.
For the husband is the head of the wife, just as Christ is the head of the church, his body, of which he is the Saviour.
So just as the church follows Christ, wives should follow their husbands in everything.
Husbands, love your wives, just as Christ loved the church and gave himself up for her,
to make her holy, cleaning her by washing her with water through the word.
He did this to present the church to himself as glorious — with no stain or wrinkle or any such thing, but holy and without fault.
In the same way, husbands should love their wives as they love their own bodies. A man who loves his wife loves himself.
No one ever hates his own body. Instead, he feeds and cares for it, just as Christ cares for the church,
because we are parts of his body.
As the Scripture says: For this reason a man will leave his father and mother and be joined to his wife, and the two will become one flesh.
This is a deep secret — but I am talking about Christ and the church.
So each one of you also must love his wife as he loves himself, and the wife must respect her husband.

6.source: Drafted from the public-domain King James Version in the simple-English style of this Bible.

verses.text: Children, obey your parents because you belong to the Lord, for this is right.
Honor your father and mother. This is the first command that comes with a promise:
so that things will go well with you, and you will live a long time on the earth.
Fathers, do not make your children angry. Instead, bring them up with the teaching and training of the Lord.
Servants, obey your earthly masters with respect and care, and with an honest heart, as if you were obeying Christ.
Do not obey only when they are watching, to win their favor. Instead, work as servants of Christ, doing what God wants with all your heart.
Serve with a good attitude, as if you were serving the Lord and not people.
Remember that the Lord will reward each person for the good he does, whether he is a slave or free.
Masters, treat your servants in the same good way. Do not threaten them. Remember that the Master of both you and them is in heaven, and he treats everyone the same.
Finally, be strong in the Lord and in his mighty power.
Put on the full armor of God, so that you can stand against the tricks of the devil.
For our fight is not against people. It is against rulers, against authorities, against the powers of this dark world, and against evil spirits in the heavenly places.
So put on the full armor of God, so that when the evil day comes, you will be able to stand your ground, and after you have done everything, to keep standing.
So stand firm. Wear the belt of truth, and the chest-armor of right living.
On your feet, wear the readiness that comes from the good news of peace.
Most important of all, take up the shield of faith. With it you can stop all the burning arrows of the evil one.
Take the helmet of salvation, and the sword of the Spirit, which is the word of God.
Pray in the Spirit at all times, with every kind of prayer and request. Stay alert, and keep on praying for all God's people.
And pray for me too, that whenever I speak, God will give me the right words, so that I can boldly make known the secret of the good news.
I am its messenger, even though I am in chains. Pray that I may tell it boldly, as I should.
Tychicus will tell you everything, so that you also may know how I am and what I am doing. He is a dear brother and a faithful worker in the Lord.
I am sending him to you for this very reason: so that you may know how we are, and so that he may encourage your hearts.
May God the Father and the Lord Jesus Christ give peace to the brothers and sisters, and love together with faith.
May grace be with all who love our Lord Jesus Christ with a love that never dies. Amen.
